steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence has an independent clause on the \left(‘The Suzuki method is an approach to music instruction’) and a relative clause beginning with that on the right. Inside that relative clause, both an -ing phrase and the main verb emphasizes must fit correctly.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — that, is aiming: disrupts the flow of the relative clause and leaves the -ing phrase improperly placed. ✗
  • Choice B — that, aiming: ‘that, aiming to teach music in the same natural way children learn language, emphasizes…’ — the -ing phrase is properly bracketed, leaving emphasizes as the clear main verb of the relative clause. ✓
  • Choice C — that aims: creates a run-on structure inside the relative clause. ✗
  • Choice D — that has aimed: same structural error as C. ✗
B is correct.
